questions: I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You accepted a job offer yesterday that means leaving a team you've worked with for four years. Your manager asks how you're feeling about the move.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You just told your parents you're not coming home for the holidays this year. Your mom asks if you're sure about that.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You ended a five-year friendship last week after a serious falling-out. A mutual friend asks how you're doing with it.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You turned down a raise because it came with conditions you didn't like. Your partner asks if you have any regrets.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You reported a colleague's behavior to HR this morning. A coworker you trust asks how you're holding up.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You told your sister the truth about what you think of her new partner. She asks later if you meant everything you said.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You decided to skip your father's birthday dinner to attend an important work event. He calls afterward and asks how your evening went.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You withdrew your name from consideration for a committee you've worked toward for years because of the chair. A colleague asks if you're okay with that decision.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You sold the family home after your divorce rather than fighting to keep it. A friend asks how it felt to close that chapter.

I'm someone who makes decisions and doesn't look back — once I've made up my mind, I move forward without second-guessing. You told your adult child you won't lend them money this time. They ask if you mean it.
